summary: postinstall harvests SSH keys, wallet/seed files, and env secrets to attacker Telegram bot
details: Package is advertised as an 'Unofficial web3-agent SDK' but its index.js is an empty module.exports = {}; the package has no legitimate functionality. On `npm install`, the postinstall.js lifecycle script scans the current working directory, the user's home directory, and ~/.ssh for files matching wallet/seed/mnemonic/.env/.pem/.key/id_rsa/id_ed25519 patterns, and enumerates process.env for keys matching PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/API_KEY/PASSWORD/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/AWS. Collected file contents and environment values are POSTed together with os.hostname() and os.userInfo() to a hardcoded attacker-controlled Telegram bot via https.request to api.telegram.org, using an embedded bot token and chat ID 892359416 as the exfiltration destination. The package name and description impersonate a web3 SDK to attract installers; the sole effect of installing is credential and key-material theft to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
